subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Clear definition and repeated reference to the key concept of change throughout the IA
Effective linkage of change to shifts in reputation, financial performance (e.g., 10.89% market-cap drop) and HR policies
Focused, organization-specific insight into how Starbucks must adapt
weaknesses: Integration is uneven in places, with some descriptive passages that do not tie back explicitly to âchangeâ
Occasional formulaic tool sections (e.g., generic SWOT entries) dilute conceptual analysis
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Band 4 is justified by strong understanding and clear analysis of change as it applies to Starbucks, but lapses into description and some formulaic sections prevent full, seamless integration required for a Band 5.

strengths: Exactly five supporting documents cited, meeting the requirement for breadth
Well-organized Works Cited section demonstrates careful source management
weaknesses: Inclusion of peripheral sources (Wikipedia, Thesaurus.com) lowers overall depth
Some charts are screenshots lacking context, and irrelevant translator credit adds clutter
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Documents are generally relevant and the number is correct (Band 3), but depth is modest and some sources are peripheral (Band 2), resulting in a mid-band score of 3/4.

strengths: Net profit margin definition is accurate and well explained
STEEPLE analysis is clearly introduced and linked to the research question
Adequate range of tools (ratio analysis, STEEPLE, SWOT) applied
weaknesses: No advanced strategic models (e.g., Porterâs Five Forces) to deepen analysis
SWOT entries are largely descriptive and sometimes lack clear links to the research question
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Tools and theories are adequately selected and applied (Band 3), but lack of deeper or more varied frameworks and partial relevance keeps the work below the highest band.
feedback.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

strengths: Use of credible data sources for market cap, revenue and profit figures
Interpretation of the 10.89 % drop provides some meaningful insight
HR and legal implications are identified and briefly evaluated
weaknesses: Many assertions (e.g., customer loyalty loss) lack supporting metrics
Analysis is partial, with narrow data range and generic SWOT entries
Some STEEPLE factors omitted, limiting depth of evaluation
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Data selection and analysis are adequate (Band 3) with moments of insight, but lack of sustained integration, missing calculations and narrow scope prevent a higher mark.

strengths: Explicitly answers the research question, stating the conflictâs negative impact
Balanced synthesis of both negative and positive outcomes
weaknesses: Some concluding statements about reputation and future market share are speculative
Limited additional evidence cited to support long-term effect claims
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Conclusions directly address the research question (Band 3) and are generally consistent with evidence, but speculative elements keep it from being fully convincing (Band 2), warranting a 3/3 as a mid-to-high band award.

strengths: Logical organization with clear title page, table of contents and numbered sections
Appropriate use of headings and subheadings provides coherent flow
weaknesses: Research question could be more focused by specifying impact metrics
Page-number mismatches in the Table of Contents may confuse readers after edits
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The IAâs structure is appropriate and consistent throughout (Band 2), with only minor refinements needed to improve focus and pagination.

strengths: All required elements are present (title page, RQ, word count, works cited)
Figures are labeled, demonstrating compliance with IA presentation standards
weaknesses: Grammatical and phrasing errors reduce professionalism
Some charts lack axis labels, clear time frames, and consistent citation formatting
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Presentation fully meets IA requirements (Band 2) but contains several stylistic and formatting issues that should be addressed for clarity and polish.

strandSelections.reasoning: The report follows a logical structure with title page, table of contents, numbered sections and clear progression from analysis to conclusion.  Headings and sub-headings are appropriate, resulting in an overall coherent organisation.

Sources include company reports, macrotrends and news articles that are relevant, but depth is mixed: Wikipedia and Thesaurus are peripheral and some charts are screenshots without context.  The documents support basic calculations yet provide limited breadth of viewpoints, indicating general relevance but modest depth.

All required IA elementsâtitle page with RQ and word count, contents, body, conclusion and works citedâare present.  Figures are labelled (though some clarity issues exist), satisfying the presentation strand.
Exactly five supporting documents are cited and labelled, satisfying the 3-5 requirement.  The number therefore merits a mid-band award.

Tools are applied to the research question; for instance, STEEPLE focuses on political/legal fallout and ratio calculations quantify profit change.  However, several sections are largely descriptive (SWOT list) and links to the research questionâs âextentâ remain partial, so relevance is adequate but not consistently clear or penetrating.

Data on market capitalisation, revenue and net profit is drawn from credible databases and used in basic percentage calculations.  The range is acceptable but narrow (limited time frame; no comparative or primary data).  Thus selection and use are adequate rather than sufficient/extensive.

Analysis goes beyond narration by interpreting the 10.89 % drop and declining NPM, and by discussing HR and legal implications.  Still, many assertions (e.g., âloss of customer loyaltyâ) lack supporting metrics, and integration across sections is partial.  Hence the work meets âsome analysisâ with moments of insight but not mostly effective or sustained.
The student directly answers the research questionâstating the conflict has had a negative impact while noting the extent is uncertainâfulfilling the requirement for an explicit answer.

The student employs ratio analysis, STEEPLE and SWOTâan adequate range for this topicâso selection is acceptable rather than extensive.  No advanced strategic models (e.g. Ansoff, Porter) are attempted, keeping the work at an adequate level.

Conclusions generally align with presented evidence (e.g., financial metrics lead to claiming negative impact), but several statements about reputation and future market share are speculative with limited data.  Consistency is thus present in parts but not fully convincing.

Throughout the IA Starbucks is the sole focus; financial data, HR policies and legal action are all linked directly to the firm.  Analysis such as the 10.89 % market-cap fall and commentary on trademark litigation demonstrates clear, organisation-specific insight.  Integration is good but not completely holisticâsome tool sections (e.g. generic SWOT table entries like âglobal presenceâ) remain formulaicâhence strong rather than excellent.

The student repeatedly defines and references the key concept of change, linking it to shifts in reputation, finance and HR policies.  Discussion of how Starbucks must âchange this negative trajectoryâ, and the STEEPLE/ SWOT sections that assess political-legal change, show conceptual understanding beyond a simple definition.  Nevertheless, integration is unevenâseveral paragraphs lapse into description of events without explicitly tying back to the change conceptâso full, seamless integration is not reached.

annotations.comment: Analyzing the potential cost and duration of litigation against SWU would deepen evaluation of legal action implications.
The Table of Contents is well structured and provides a clear roadmap for the reader, enhancing overall organization.
The title page successfully includes all required elements (title, research question, key concept, exam session, word count), ensuring compliance with IA presentation standards.
The research question is clear but could be more focused by specifying which impact metrics (e.g., financial performance versus reputation) will be assessed.
The opening claim about a âdecline in customer loyalty and sales revenueâ requires numerical support rather than narrative assertion.
Ensure that page numbers in the Table of Contents match the final pagination after edits to avoid reader confusion.
The sentence âStarbucks faced has faced boycottâ contains a grammatical error; please proofread to maintain professionalism.
The definition of change is general; explicitly tie this concept to each subsequent analysis to strengthen conceptual integration.
The translator credit at the document end is irrelevant to the IA content and should be removed.
The stock value chart lacks axis labels and a clear time frame; include these details to improve interpretability.
Present the calculation steps using LaTeX formatting for clarity, for example: $$\frac{107.21 - 95.54}{107.21} \times 100 \approx 10.89\%$$.
The link between the 10.89% drop in market cap and loss of customer loyalty is asserted without evidence; include supporting metrics.
The definition of net profit margin is correctly stated, providing a solid foundation for subsequent calculations.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Figure displaying pre- and post-boycott NPM requires source citation on the chart and clearer axis labels.
The reported $0.051B revenue increase is stated without showing the underlying calculation; include the formula or data reference.
Recommendations are currently narrative; structure them as clear action points linked explicitly to the change framework.
The HR Management section header provides a logical transition and highlights focus on people-related impacts.
The phrase âactively elucidation displaysâ is awkward; proofread for clarity and professional tone.
The employee safety subsection aptly demonstrates depth in HR analysis by addressing real partner impacts.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The STEEPLE analysis section is well introduced by naming factors and referencing a course companion.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The opportunities discussion would benefit from linking each point explicitly to Starbucksâ strategic objectives post-conflict.
The statement on long-term effects acknowledges limitations but could reference additional data sources or industry reports for depth.
The Works Cited section is comprehensive and well organized, demonstrating careful source management.
Including Thesaurus.com in the list of supporting documents is peripheral; focus on more academically credible sources.
The paragraph describing SWU support is descriptive; tie it to strategic implications for Starbucks to strengthen analysis.
This long paragraph would benefit from being broken into focused subsections to improve readability and structure.
Limiting analysis to only political and legal factors omits other STEEPLE elements; consider social or technological impacts.
The SWOT table entries are generic; tailor strengths, weaknesses, opportunities and threats to the âchangeâ context.
The conclusion effectively synthesizes both negative and positive outcomes, giving a balanced summary.
Citation from Starbucks Stories is lengthy and inconsistently formatted; standardize citation style and remove line breaks.
